beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check shop inventory spreadsheet on phone
beats.reasoning: Jackson wakes early as usual; low energy from yesterday (40/100) means he stays in routine mode, checking his meticulous spreadsheet before the day demands anything. Transit strike means he'll need to drive to Renton instead of relying on transit.

beats.activity: Drive to work in Renton; transit strike forces car commute
beats.reasoning: Metro strike eliminates bus/rail; Jackson drives instead, adding ~15 min to normal commute. Mild weather and early start mean smooth traffic.

beats.activity: Bike mechanic work: brake adjustments, derailleur tuning, customer repairs
beats.reasoning: Morning shift; Jackson is methodical and rhythm-driven with his repair work, so he settles into the familiar groove of wrenches and adjustments.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and water at shop or nearby
beats.reasoning: Jackson stays on-site for lunch; low energy and transit disruption make him less likely to wander. Keeps hydration in mind given yesterday's heat reminder.

beats.activity: Afternoon bike mechanic work: chain maintenance, spoke truing, customer service
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift continues; mild weather and low energy mean Jackson stays focused on his work without distraction or early exit.

beats.activity: Drive home to Northgate; transit strike still in effect but easing toward 4pm end time
beats.reasoning: Strike ends at 4pm but Jackson leaves at 5:30pm, so normal traffic patterns resume. Drive back north takes ~50 min in early evening.

beats.activity: Water garden, check plant progress, pull any ripe vegetables
beats.reasoning: Jackson's ritual: urban gardening is a core homebody pleasure. Mild, partly cloudy weather is perfect for tending plants without heat stress.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home; review daily expenses in spreadsheet
beats.reasoning: Low energy from yesterday keeps Jackson home and introspective. He enjoys his meticulous expense tracking as a meditative activity, fitting his homebody nature.

beats.activity: Relax at home: read, listen to music, or watch something low-key
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat; Jackson is easily distracted but values quiet time at home, especially after a full work day. Low energy means no urge to socialize tonight.
Jackson gets a solid 8.5 hours of sleep, recovering from yesterday's energy dip. Sleep ends well before 6am the next calendar day.

diary: Woke up early to check inventory before heading to the shop—the transit strike meant I had to drive instead of taking my usual bike route. Funny irony, considering what I do for a living. The drive to Renton wasn't too bad, actually quieter than usual. Spent the morning sorting out brake adjustments and a tricky derailleur that had a customer frustrated. Grabbed a sandwich around lunch and kept moving. Afternoon was steady work—chain maintenance, spoke truing, the usual rhythm. By the time I headed home around three, the strike was winding down. Got the garden watered when I got back; the tomatoes are really coming in now. Made a simple dinner, reviewed expenses, then just sat with some music and a book. Nothing fancy, but it felt earned.
thought_process: The transit strike threw me off initially—I pride myself on biking when possible, so having to drive felt like a small defeat. But I rolled with it; that's the mechanic mindset. Work was solid and kept me occupied, which was good. I was thinking about the weekend ride I'm guiding on Sunday, so part of my brain was already there. Dinner was routine, expenses were stable. The day had that therapeutic quality where physical work and tending the garden balance each other out. No stress, just the steady hum of a regular Thursday. By evening I was genuinely tired but satisfied.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
